author.description: Retired Border Patrol Commander-at-Large. Spent 30 years doing the job politicians only campaign on. Now calling out their failures and offering real solutions.

tweets.text:
Apprehension numbers are so low they’re cooking the books — straight Obama style.

I just called out Homan downplaying a 12% drop in ICE arrests as a “minor hiccup.” The real pipelines for mass deportations (fear of removal, self-deportations) are bone dry. There is no total-spectrum enforcement. There are no mass deportations. Just soft-target theater.

Now the White House suddenly brags they’ve “deported” 800,000. How? By pulling the exact metric trick Republicans used to roast Obama for — lumping in border turnaways and removals.

Same game Homan ran to earn his Obama Presidential Rank Award in 2015. Same scoreboard, same “distinguished” results on paper.

Manipulation 101: when the actual game isn’t delivering, you change how you keep score.

Without raw fear and full-spectrum enforcement, these are still empty numbers. 

The country doesn’t need rebranded stats. It needs results at scale.
Genius tactical move or just more slow-walk?

author.description: Retired Border Patrol Commander-at-Large. Spent 30 years doing the job politicians only campaign on. Now calling out their failures and offering real solutions.

tweets.text:
Congressman Castro’s playing the classic empathy trap.  

Painting ICE as “rogue abusers” for detaining a Venezuelan family at a school bus stop. Dramatic optics, for sure, but misleading as hell…

Fact: This family entered on parole in 2021, had pending asylum claims, but no permanent legal status per DHS. ICE enforced the law. 

The system working is working here, not “tyranny.”

Local Texas cops aren’t “turned into ICE agents.” They only engage on immigration when criminal conduct brings someone into custody—standard 287(g) cooperation for public safety. 

Castro wants sanctuary rules that shield criminal violators thus eroding public trust.

Meanwhile, budgets are crushed by immigrant welfare use, education, healthcare, and housing for households without full status. 

Taxpayers foot the bill while services are taken from American citizens. That’s not compassion, and it is unsustainable.

Castro’s selective story hides the real game.  Weak enforcement creates chaos, divided communities, and forces locals to clean up the mess. Americans want laws followed, not selective laws and emotional theater. 

Enforce borders. Prioritize citizens. Accountability over anecdotes.

Rule of law isn’t abuse. It’s the foundation.

Mass deportations are the solution!

author.description: Retired Border Patrol Commander-at-Large. Spent 30 years doing the job politicians only campaign on. Now calling out their failures and offering real solutions.

tweets.text:
The American mind has been expertly hypnotized.

We imported millions from countries where “driving” means chaotic survival rules, no lanes, no signals, horns as brakes. Then we acted shocked when they bring those habits here—CDL in hand, fresh off the illegal entry.

Now four Indian illegals with commercial licenses just racked up multiple deadly wrecks killing Americans. And the pattern repeats.

Targeted enforcement? Good luck. Most won’t have rap sheets. They’re not “criminals” by the narrow definition we use. 

They’re just drivers operating at Third World competence levels on American roads.

We conditioned ourselves to call this “diversity” and “vibrancy” while the body count climbs.

Normalizing lower standards doesn’t raise anyone up. It drags safety, competence, and lives down.

Wake up. Your family’s next on the highway.

author.description: Retired Border Patrol Commander-at-Large. Spent 30 years doing the job politicians only campaign on. Now calling out their failures and offering real solutions.

tweets.text:
Throwback Thursday. 

This is the scene outside of a refugee resettlement church/shelter, in El Paso, during the Biden administration, which sadly,  we probably funded.

Look at this fine group of gentlemen swarming a car, pounding on the windows, yanking door handles, and throwing rocks. 

Are these the type of people we should be allowing into the country?

Flash forward to today. 

Where are they now? Unknown. 

If they have not been arrested since then, these are the kinda guys that would likely be missed in a “targeted” operation. 

This is why the only answer is mass deportations with full spectrum enforcement.  

Don’t be coy with mass deportations!

tweets.text:
Rep. Chip Roy is 100% right!

Freeze ALL immigration and start mass deportations of the millions of ILLEGAL ALIENS who shouldn’t be here, PERIOD.

Since every single issue we face including wages, housing, crime, schools, hospitals, welfare, and national security is downstream of uncontrolled immigration ( legal and illegal), it’s absolutely baffling that most Republicans still aren’t fully in line.

The winning message is? Full stop.

Everything else sells out our country and royally screws our own people. 

Voters already get it, but many politicians ( RINOs like Tillis,  for example) and liberals don’t.

Time to lead or get out of the way. 🇺🇸

author.description: Retired Border Patrol Commander-at-Large. Spent 30 years doing the job politicians only campaign on. Now calling out their failures and offering real solutions.

tweets.text:
Tom Homan calls a 12% drop in deportations a “minor hiccup.” What he’s not telling you is local police are flooding into the 287(g) program — that’s the real pipeline to ICE.  The rest are sprinkled targets that were hit. 

The Mean Green team hit a hundred apprehensions a day on roving patrols. This was just with a few hundred agents. 

That fear alone drove two million to self-deport. Check CBP home app numbers for yourself. You can see it now. The CBP Home app has gone dead. It’s not used. 

Homan’s approach creates zero fear. It’s a drop in the bucket against the scale we’re facing-100 million. 

There’s no path through this except total-spectrum enforcement and mass deportations. 

Numbers without fear are just theater.

tweets.text:
All issues are downstream of immigration. Look how this web ties together…

The Wall Street Apes post hits hard. 

California’s got over 5.5 million people on EBT, yet starting June 1st, the feds are enforcing work requirements that could cut off hundreds of thousands of Americans for not working 24 hours a week. 

Meanwhile, the state keeps pouring resources into supporting non-citizens through its own programs.

California has 10.9 million foreign-born residents — 28% of the entire state, more than double the national average.

This directly drives your insane cost of housing. 

All those extra people competing for limited housing stock sends rents and home prices through the roof. Same with traffic.  Way more cars on the same roads. 

And imagine the burden on those teachers just trying to get these kids caught up and speaking English. How does that affect your kids when they’re trying to get a basic education?

One root cause, rippling out everywhere. 

Immigration shapes it all.  

Mass deportations will solve all of this instantly!
